intro: Backblaze B2 enters at $0 against Amazon S3 at $5.87 per month. Both tools position themselves for image/video heavy applications, so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Amazon S3 — Object storage service with 11 nines durability, S3 Select, versioning, and lifecycle management. Backblaze B2 — S3-compatible object storage at $0.006/GB with free egress to Cloudflare CDN partners. Amazon S3 scores 5/5 on developer experience versus 4/5 for Backblaze B2. Backblaze B2 rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Amazon S3. Both tools list image/video heavy applications as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including image/video heavy applications. The honest trade-off: Amazon S3 is not built for small projects, and Backblaze B2 is not the right call for small projects, so neither is a universal default. Transparency scores land at 4/5 for Amazon S3 and 5/5 for Backblaze B2, which reflects how openly each tool documents pricing, limits, and terms.
quick_take: Amazon S3 is for image/video heavy applications; Backblaze B2 overlaps on the same job; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Amazon S3 if image/video heavy applications and the $5.87 per month entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Backblaze B2 if image/video heavy applications and the $0 entry price fits your budget.

faq.q: Is Amazon S3 cheaper than Backblaze B2?
faq.a: Based on the listed entry prices, no: Amazon S3 starts at $5.87 per month while Backblaze B2 starts at $0. At low volume the gap may be small, but it widens fast at scale. Always price against your own usage curve, not the headline tier.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amazon S3 to Backblaze B2?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Amazon S3 is moderate effort at medium lock-in (S3 API became de facto standard; data egress costly), and onboarding to Backblaze B2 involves medium lock-in (S3-compatible, portable). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Amazon S3 or Backblaze B2?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, Amazon S3 rates 5/5 while Backblaze B2 rates 4/5, giving Amazon S3 the edge. Scores compress a lot of variance though — SDK language coverage, error messages, and docs quality matter more than the number. Run a quick integration spike on both before trusting the gap.

faq.q: Is Backblaze B2 a good alternative to Amazon S3?
faq.a: Yes, Backblaze B2 targets the same job Amazon S3 does — image/video heavy applications — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (subscription from $0 vs subscription from $5.87 per month) and lock-in (medium lock-in (S3-compatible, portable) vs medium lock-in (S3 API became de facto standard; data egress costly)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Backblaze B2 is explicitly not for small projects.
